office_does: A state senator writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: A long-serving senator emphasizing housing, infrastructure, and civil protections faces a former councilmember emphasizing lower taxes, spending restraint, public safety, and parental rights.

candidates.summary: Liias is a long-serving state senator and transportation chair supporting housing and infrastructure investment, targeted tax relief, and LGBTQ and immigrant protections.
highlights: Supports affordable housing near transit, infrastructure, tax relief, and a working-family tax credit [S1]
Supports childcare and LGBTQ and immigrant protections [S1]
Reports securing housing resources and chairing transportation investment [S1]

taxes.basis: He supports targeted small-business tax relief and a working-family tax credit.

spending.basis: He supports and reports a record of housing and transportation investment.

housing.basis: He supports affordable housing near transit and reports securing housing resources.

experience.basis: He has served in the Senate since 2014 after service as a representative and councilmember.

immigration.basis: He explicitly supports protections for immigrants.

candidates.summary: Khan is a former councilmember, labor representative, and business founder emphasizing lower taxes, spending restraint, public safety, and parental rights.
highlights: Supports lower taxes, spending restraint, and transparency [S1]
Supports public safety, affordable housing, education, and parental rights [S1]

spending.basis: He explicitly supports spending restraint and transparency.

safety.basis: He makes public safety a priority, though the dossier provides no detailed enforcement proposal.

experience.basis: He combines former city-council service with private-sector and labor experience outside the Legislature.
